node --test test/global-runtime.test.js.summary: compatibility check for existing add/list/remove/scan and global consumers

failures: project add created legacy/no v2 payload shape
legacy v1 read/migration was not bridged through CLI/global-runtime
project repair route failed before implementation
unsupported newer v2 registry was not surfaced through state_version_unsupported

covered: project add creates LAUNCHDECK_HOME/registry/projects.json as v2
legacy LAUNCHDECK_HOME/projects.json is read when v2 is absent and migrated on mutation
alias repair preserves projectId and alias collisions emit alias_conflict with existingProjectId
unsupported newer registry version emits state_version_unsupported with repair next action
project repair updates alias/projectRoot/configPath while preserving projectId

concerns: No lsp_diagnostics tool is exposed in this runtime; Node syntax checks and targeted tests were used for verification.
git status reports the touched source paths as untracked from the current git root, so git diff did not provide tracked-file patch output.
behavior_surfaces: JSON failure envelope data mirrors for CLI errors
state_contracts: Global project registry reads v2 from LAUNCHDECK_HOME/registry/projects.json first.
Global project registry reads legacy v1 LAUNCHDECK_HOME/projects.json only when v2 is absent.
Mutations write only the v2 registry/projects.json path.
v2 project records preserve projectId plus legacy id/key/name mirrors for existing consumers.
project add is idempotent by projectRoot/key and rejects alias conflicts for other projectIds.
project repair changes alias/projectRoot/configPath while preserving projectId.
Unsupported newer registry versions surface state_version_unsupported with foundVersion, supportedVersion, statePath, and repair guidance.
confidence_notes: Scope stayed within the allowed source files plus this result file.
No tests, docs, locks, events, runtime files, package metadata, or feature workflow artifacts were edited.
